CLM-PLAY-001-01.text: TPG acquired Angelo Gordon for ~$2.7B (April-November 2023), adding $73B AUM and reaching $303B by end-2025.

CLM-PLAY-001-02.text: The AUM addition moved TPG toward a higher DVR-001 quartile and is consistent with improved P/FRE positioning.

CLM-PLAY-002-01.text: BAM spun off from BN in December 2022 and completed full AM internalization in December 2025, quadrupling directly attributable FRE.

CLM-PLAY-002-02.text: Corporate simplification made BAM's scale advantage visible to public market investors, and may have contributed to P/FRE re-rating.

CLM-PLAY-003-01.text: Apollo's Athene full merger (January 2022) consolidated $210B insurance AUM, growing to $300B+ by end-2025.

CLM-PLAY-003-02.text: Insurance float integration appears to have accelerated AUM growth beyond what LP fundraising alone would have supported, consistent with the DVR-001 scale premium.

CLM-ANTI-001-01.text: BAM traded at a conglomerate discount before the 2022 spin-off despite commanding $1T+ AUM.

CLM-PLAY-004-01.text: Ares expanded European direct lending 2022-2025, raising EDL IV at EUR 10B+ and reaching $407B credit AUM.

CLM-PLAY-004-02.text: European expansion appears to have driven Q1 FRE growth positioning, which is consistent with exploratory DVR-002 evidence that high FRE growth suppresses trailing EV/FEAUM while priming forward multiple recovery.

CLM-ANTI-002-01.text: Carlyle achieved record FRE ($1.24B, 47% margin) under Schwartz but maintained Q4 FRE growth quartile, trading at a P/FRE discount to Apollo and KKR.

CLM-PLAY-005-01.text: Apollo's permanent capital exceeds 62% of total AUM (driven by Athene); BAM's is 87% long-dated or perpetual.

CLM-PLAY-005-02.text: Permanent capital appears to contribute to P/FRE premium through earnings quality improvement, consistent with the BH-significant DVR-003 finding.

CLM-PLAY-006-01.text: KKR K-Series grew from ~$10B (2022) to $35B+ (2025); StepStone SPW reached $8B+ in 2.5 years from near-zero.

CLM-PLAY-006-02.text: Wealth-channel expansion appears to improve FEAUM positioning and is associated with margin-accretive fee rate premiums, consistent with DVR-003 evidence.

CLM-ANTI-003-01.text: BREIT faced redemption gate activation in late 2022 following redemption requests exceeding 2% of NAV quarterly limits.

CLM-PLAY-007-01.text: Blue Owl has 85% permanent capital across its platform with $12.9B in GP Strategic Capital Fund V and 50+ GP stakes.

CLM-PLAY-007-02.text: GP minority stakes may contribute to P/DE re-rating through permanent capital quality improvement, consistent with exploratory DVR-004 evidence — though this causal link is directional only and not statistically confirmed in this cross-section.

CLM-PLAY-008-01.text: Carlyle's credit AUM surpassed PE ($211B vs $164B) and FRE margin improved from ~40% to 47% under Schwartz (2023-2025).

CLM-PLAY-008-02.text: Credit mix rotation appears to have improved G&A/FEAUM positioning, consistent with the exploratory DVR-005 negative correlation — though BH correction failure means this directional relationship is not confirmed.

CLM-PLAY-C001-01.text: Ares expanded European direct lending to $75B+ AUM with EDL IV at EUR 10B+; credit AUM reached $407B by end-2025.

CLM-PLAY-C001-02.text: European expansion appears to have driven FEAUM improvement consistent with DVR-003's BH-significant P/FRE association, though the geographic contribution is not separately isolable.

CLM-PLAY-C002-01.text: ARCC is the world's largest publicly traded BDC at $22B+ NAV, providing Ares with permanent capital for direct lending.

CLM-PLAY-C002-02.text: BDC permanent capital may contribute to P/DE premium through earnings quality improvement, consistent with the exploratory DVR-004 directional evidence — though the causal link is not statistically confirmed.

CLM-PLAY-PE001-01.text: TPG's Angelo Gordon acquisition (November 2023) tripled AUM to $303B by end-2025 and drove FRE to $953M (+25%) at 45% margin.

CLM-PLAY-PE001-02.text: The AUM addition appears to have moved TPG toward a higher DVR-001 quartile, consistent with the BH-significant scale-to-P/FRE association.

CLM-PLAY-PE002-01.text: PAX acquired iGah3 in 2024 (ACT-VD-049), entering growth equity as a fourth PE sub-type.

CLM-PLAY-PE002-02.text: Growth equity addition appears to support FRE growth trajectory consistent with DVR-002 exploratory evidence, though the FRE contribution from iGah3 specifically is not yet separately disclosed.

CLM-ANTI-PE001-01.text: Median LP co-investment of 33 cents per $1 commingled commitment translates to approximately 25% fee revenue reduction at fund level.

CLM-PLAY-INF001-01.text: BAM's Super-Core Infrastructure is an open-end vehicle targeting pension/insurance permanent capital. 87% of BAM's total AUM is long-dated or perpetual.

CLM-PLAY-INF001-02.text: Open-end infrastructure vehicles may contribute to P/DE premium through permanent capital quality improvement, consistent with exploratory DVR-004 evidence — though the causal link is directional only and not statistically confirmed.

CLM-PLAY-INF002-01.text: TPG Rise Climate Fund I raised $7.3B (largest dedicated climate PE fund globally); BAM committed $100B+ to transition infrastructure.

CLM-PLAY-INF002-02.text: Thematic energy transition positioning appears to provide access to LP categories unavailable through traditional alt-manager channels, supporting AUM growth consistent with DVR-001.

CLM-PLAY-RE001-01.text: Brazilian FII market represents $60B+ in listed real estate funds with 2M+ retail investors, providing a regulatory framework for open-end RE vehicles.

CLM-PLAY-RE001-02.text: A PAX FII may contribute to permanent capital % improvement consistent with the exploratory DVR-004 directional evidence, though the causal link between permanent capital and P/DE premium is not statistically confirmed.

CLM-PLAY-RE002-01.text: The RE credit segment (construction lending, bridge loans, sale-leaseback) has grown across multiple alt-asset managers as banks retreat from these activities; the market for non-bank RE credit is estimated at $50B+ annually in the US and Europe.

CLM-PLAY-RE002-02.text: RE credit expansion appears to improve FEAUM positioning consistent with DVR-003's BH-significant P/FRE association, though causal attribution to RE credit specifically requires hedging.

CLM-ANTI-RE001-01.text: BREIT faced redemption gate activation in late 2022 following redemption requests exceeding 2% of NAV quarterly limits; episode resolved by 2024-2025.

CLM-PLAY-GPS001-01.text: Blue Owl has 85% permanent capital, $12.9B in GP Strategic Capital Fund V, and stakes in 50+ alt managers.

CLM-PLAY-GPS001-02.text: GP minority stakes FEAUM growth appears to support DVR-001 AUM scale improvement consistent with the BH-significant scale-to-P/FRE association — though the quality-adjusted contribution of permanent capital FEAUM specifically is not separately confirmed.

CLM-PLAY-GPS002-01.text: StepStone's Cobalt platform covers 50,000+ funds and is licensed to third-party institutional investors; SPW grew to $8B+ in 2.5 years from near-zero.

CLM-PLAY-GPS002-02.text: Technology commercialization appears to contribute to FRE growth, consistent with exploratory DVR-002 evidence — though the causal relationship between technology revenue and EV/FEAUM compression is not confirmed.

CLM-ANTI-GPS001-01.text: Blue Owl and KKR/Arctos command an estimated 60-70% of deployed GP stakes capital; Petershill Partners delisted in December 2025.
